problem: A pipe with inside diameter 10'' is to carry water from a reservoir to a small town in an arid land. Neglecting the friction and turbulence of the water against the inside of the pipes, what is the minimum number of 2''-inside-diameter pipes of the same length needed to carry the same volume of water to the arid town?

classified_questions.question: How would you calculate the volume of a 10-inch pipe?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What information do you need to determine the number of 2-inch pipes needed to match the volume of one 10-inch pipe?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What is the formula for calculating the volume of a 2-inch pipe?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How do you compare the volumes of a 10-inch pipe and a 2-inch pipe?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why is it necessary to neglect friction and turbulence in this problem?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What other factors could affect the number of 2-inch pipes needed in this scenario?
classified_questions.label: b
